msgs.text:
Turn 3 — restricted mod-2 tail with section twist included.I re-read the three attachments from this message only. The problem is still the over-G isomorphism question for the two quotients defined by rS and rH, with F=P⋊G, P the free G-operator pro-2 group on x0,x1.The previous history established that the abelianized relation rows agree after the idempotent split and the Ef-piece matrixU0=( s−11 s−22),(s+3,−2)U0=(s2+1,s−1),where s=σ2.

Turn 2 then reduced the next obstruction to the residual Ef, s=1, mod-2 Magnus tail, explicitly warning that section twists must be included.The main new point is that the residual calculation must be treated as a restricted / Bockstein Magnus calculation, not just as an ordinary linear substitution. The entry 2 in U0 is invisible after reducing the abelianized matrix mod 2, but any actual pro-2 lift of a 2B abelianized term contributes a forced B2 -term in the mod-2 Magnus expansion. This is exactly the kind of structural pattern that a one-case computation would miss.

On the dangerous Ef-piece, Turn 2 reduced the relators toRS=(x0s)−1x0−3x12(x1,x1s),RH=x0s2x0x1−1x1s,and used RS−1.Now set s=1 and work in the completed mod-2 Magnus algebra withX=x0,Y=x1,X=1+A,Y=1+B.

A section twist by q means that every residual Zs is read as Zq=q−1 Zq. Thus the residual source expression isS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)−1Y1−2Y03Y0q,while the residual target is simplyRH≡X2=1+A2.

A naïve lift of U0 produces a cubic Bockstein tail. But it is not invariant: it disappears once the allowed section twist is used. Let

C:=(X,Y)=X−1Y−1XY,and take the residual liftY0:=YC,Y1:=CX−1Y2,q:=Y.

This has the correct residual abelianized shape:[Y0]=B,[Y1]=−A+2B,matching the s=1 specialization of the two columns of U0. Expanding gives

C=1+(AB+BA)+O(I3),Y0=1+B+(AB+BA)+(AAB+ABA)+O(I4),Y1=1+A+(A2+AB+BA+B2)+O(I3),where I=(A,B) is the augmentation ideal. Substitution into S gives the uniform cancellationS(Y0,Y1,q)=1+A2+O(I5)=X2+O(I5).So the cubic and quartic residual obstructions vanish once section twists and the forced 2-power terms are both included.There is also a clean degree-5 cancellation. With the above Y0,Y1,q, the first remaining residual tail isΔ5=

AABAB+AABBA+ABAAB+ABABB+ABBAA+ABBAB+ABBBB+BAABA+BAABB+BABAA+BABBA+BBAAB+BBABA+BBBBA.

But this is a boundary:Δ5=AW4+W4A+BQ4+Q4B,whereW4=(AB+BA)2+B4,Q4=(AB+BA)2.These are not artificial associative-polynomial terms: (AB+BA)2 is the initial form of C2, and B4 is the initial form of Y4. Thus one may correct Y1 by a degree-4 kernel term with initial form W4, and correct the section cocycle q by a degree-4 term with initial form Q4, without disturbing the already-cleared degrees. Therefore the residual obstruction also vanishes through degree 5, equivalently

S≡RH (modI6)after these corrections.

This does not yet prove ΓS≅GΓH. The next residual tail, after one natural degree-5-clearing choice, begins in degree 6:Δ6=ABBABB+ABBBBA+BBAABB+BBABBA.I am not calling this an obstruction yet. The reason is that degree 6 can be affected by remaining lower-choice freedoms and by the full restricted Lie correction complex, not only by the minimal Y1/q corrections used above. The right next test is therefore not another isolated expansion, but the full degree-6 restricted-linear system.

ESTABLISHED: The obstruction vanishes through the residual mod-2, s=1 Magnus tail up to degree 5, once the section twist and forced pro-2 Bockstein terms are included. This strengthens Turn 2 from “quadratic cleared” to “restricted tail cleared through I6.”

GAP: No final isomorphism or non-isomorphism is proved yet. A possible degree-6 tail has appeared, but it has not been shown invariant under all remaining restricted Nielsen and cocycle freedoms.

NEXT: Build the full degree-6 restricted Lie correction complex on the residual Ef-piece and test whether

ABBABB+ABBBBA+BBAABB+BBABBA survives its image. If it survives, it is the first serious candidate for a disproof; if it dies, the same complex likely gives the inductive template for constructing the over-G isomorphism.[RESTRICTED-TAIL-DEG5-CLEARED]
